If employees may be exposed to toxic substances, or workplace conditions that present potential health hazards, IH monitoring may be conducted.
Do you perform IH monitoring on your employees?
Are company personnel exposed to hazards that would require Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring?

Harmful chemical compounds in the form of solids, liquids, gases, mists, dusts, fumes, and vapors have harmful effects by breathing, absorption through the skin, or ingestion - eating or drinking.  The degree of worker risk from exposure to any given substance depends on the nature and potency of the toxic effects and the amount and length of exposure. Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) provide you a wealth of information on how to protect your workers from chemical hazards.
Are company personnel exposed to chemical hazards during any services or operations performed?
Do you maintain Safety Data Sheets (SDSs) for all Hazardous Chemicals used, stored or maintained by employees (on- or off-site)?

These include bacteria, viruses, fungi, and other living organisms that can cause acute and chronic infections by entering the body either directly or through breaks in the skin. Occupations that deal with plants or animals or their products or with food and food processing may expose workers to biological hazards.   Proper personal hygiene is an effective measure, particularly proper attention to minor cuts and scratches, especially those on the hands and forearms, helps keep worker risks to a minimum.
Are company personnel exposed to biological hazards during any services or operations performed?
These include excessive levels of ionizing and non-ionizing electromagnetic radiation, noise, vibration, illumination, and temperature.
Are company personnel exposed to physical hazards during any services or operations performed?

Most ergonomic problems result  from poorly designed job tasks and work stations. Conditions such as excessive vibration and noise, eye strain, repetitive motion, and heavy lifting problems can cause ergonomic hazards. Improperly designed tools or work areas also can be ergonomic hazards.
Are company personnel exposed to ergonomic hazards during any services or operations performed?
These include hazards that cannot be associated with hazard types above.
